response.justification: The candidate is a strong polyglot engineer with 15+ years of experience, but his professional C++ exposure is limited to a seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 plus a thesis project, while the last decade has centered on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang for web platforms. The role demands deep, current C++/STL expertise, multi-threaded networked systems, MQTT/message-broker architectures, and IEC 62443 secure development in industrial automation — none of which appear in his recent record. The low-latency DLang work at Dunnhumby is the closest analog but does not offset the core skills gap.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests per second
Broad full-stack and embedded background with strong testing culture (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
Proven mentoring and knowledge-sharing track record through workshops, courses, and open-source projects
concerns: Professional C++ experience is minimal and roughly a decade old, versus the role's core requirement of expert-level C++/STL
No industrial automation, IoT protocol (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), or IEC 62443 / SDL secure-engineering experience
Recent career trajectory is web/product full-stack, suggesting a mismatch with an on-prem industrial C++ platform role
